title: Space-efficient change journal for a storage system
abstract: A space-efficient change journal for a storage system is provided. The storage system includes a memory having computer readable instructions stored therein. The system further includes a processor configured to access a log structure merge (LSM) tree-based metadata index having metadata for the storage system. The LSM tree-based metadata index includes indices placed in a plurality of indexing layers and one or more indices are merged within the indexing layers in response to updates to metadata, or as a background task. The processor is configured to identify one or more indices of the LSM tree-based metadata index as entries of a change journal of the storage system. The processor is further configured to maintain the change journal of the storage system based upon the identified entries.
claims: 1. A system comprising: a data repository comprising memory configured to store a plurality of files from an application executed on a computing device in accordance with a plurality of backup cycles of the application; and a metadata repository in communication with the data repository, the metadata repository configured to store a hierarchical index tree configured to store metadata of the plurality of files, the hierarchical index tree comprising a plurality of index layers, wherein the hierarchical index tree is generated by: storing, at a lower index layer of the plurality of index layers of the hierarchical index tree, a first index corresponding to a first backup cycle of the plurality of backup cycles and a second index corresponding to a second backup cycle of the plurality of backup cycles, the first index comprising a first set of metadata entries corresponding to a first set of the plurality of files and the second index comprising a second set of metadata entries corresponding to a second set of the plurality of files, wherein the second backup cycle is different from the first backup cycle; merging the first index and the second index to create a third index, wherein merging the first index and the second index comprises merging the first and second sets of metadata entries based on changes of the metadata between the first and second sets of the plurality of files occurred in between the first backup cycle and the second backup cycle; storing the third index at a higher index layer that is higher than the lower index layer in the hierarchical index tree; and garbage collecting, responsive to the first index and the second index being merged to create indexes at higher level indexing layers, wherein the garbage collecting includes determining that neither the first index nor the second index is subject to a retention policy and updating the first index and the second index in the hierarchical index tree.
